_notes.file: Replaces the former 'image' and 'markdown' primitives. Use payload.mimeType to distinguish — e.g. 'image/jpeg', 'text/markdown', 'application/pdf'. Any IANA media type is valid.
_notes.node: A generic node type for use in user-defined trees. Items of type 'node' can appear under items of type 'tree' to form named additional tree structures beyond the built-in one.
_notes.tree: A named tree root. Kanecta has one built-in tree rooted at the all-zeros UUID. Additional trees are items of type 'tree' — their children are items of type 'node'. Useful for alternative navigation structures, tag hierarchies, org charts, etc.
_notes.component: A React component definition. body lives in meta.files.body = 'body.tsx'. Props use the parameterOrProp definition — same as function parameters.
_notes.formula: A reusable calculation — used wherever a computed value is needed: alias generation, primaryField derivation, derived fields on types, display labels. Three levels of increasing power: (1) template string — simple `{variable}` interpolation (e.g. `"TASK-{n}"`); (2) DSL — Excel-like expression evaluated by formulajs (MIT), with Kanecta-provided variables and extension functions such as ITEM(uuid, field) and SEQ(typeId); (3) functionId — UUID pointer to a `function` item for full programmatic power when the DSL is not sufficient. A formula item is a standalone item with its own UUID, allowing other items to reference it stably even if the expression changes. The consumer (e.g. a type's aliasFormulas field) holds the formulaId. Built-in template variables available at all levels: `n` (auto-incrementing integer scoped per type per workspace), `year`, `month`, `date` (current date parts), `type` (type name of the item being processed), `uuid8` (first 8 characters of the new item's UUID), `field.<name>` (the value of a named field on the item being processed).
_notes.function: A typed function definition. body lives in meta.files.body = 'body.ts' (filesystem/SQLite) or inline in payload.body (Postgres).
_notes.grant: An access control grant. Lives as a child of the item it controls, in the 'grants' aspect. Supports identity, namespace, and ReBAC principals.
_notes.relationship: A typed, first-class relationship between two items. typeId points to a relationship-type item; sourceId and targetId are the connected items. Coexists with inline [[uuid]] links — use relationship items when you need a typed, confidence-carrying, or historically tracked link.
_notes.relationship-type: Defines a relationship vocabulary entry — the meta-type for relationship instances, mirroring how type defines the vocabulary for object instances. Carries meta.directional (boolean) and meta.inverse (UUID of the paired inverse relationship-type item, e.g. depends-on ↔ enables). jsonSchema validates the data payload on relationship instances of this type. Nine canonical instances are bootstrapped as system items for the standard vocabulary (relates-to, depends-on, enables, contradicts, blocks, blocked-by, prerequisite-for, derived-from, supersedes). Custom relationship types are user-defined items of type relationship-type.
_notes.view: A specific perspective on an item: the item being viewed (itemId), the renderer (componentId), and the environment (contextId). The view's UUID is a single pointer to all three. Used for bookmarking, sharing, and bootstrapping (root.payload.entryPoint points to a view item).
_notes.context: An execution and display environment. Captures runtime (web, desktop, cli, mcp, pdf) and display position (tree-node, detail-panel, modal, preview, embed). capabilities[] is exploratory — records what the runtime supports (e.g. react, css, images). Expected to evolve through use.
_notes.type: A custom type definition. The meta-type — instances of every user-defined type (object items) live under their type item as their canonical home. payload is typePayload. type items themselves live under a bootstrapper-created collection tree under root.
_notes.aspect-type: Defines the schema and semantics of a named aspect dimension — the meta-type for aspect items, mirroring how relationship-type defines the vocabulary for relationship instances. When the item.aspect field on an item is a UUID, that UUID points to an aspect-type item. The aspect-type item's item.value is the canonical string name for this dimension (e.g. 'financial-costs', 'comments', 'simplified'). payload.jsonSchema validates the payload of aspect items of this type, enabling structured queries and validation across all items that use this dimension. payload.description documents the intended use of this dimension. aspect-type items themselves live under the synthetic aspect-type type item (universal rule). Well-known aspect-type items (e.g. 'comments') may be bootstrapped as system items; custom aspect types are user-defined. The string form of the aspect field (e.g. aspect: 'comments') is the informal/unstructured version — no aspect-type item is required. The UUID form is used when structure, validation, or cross-item querying on the dimension is needed.
_notes.alias: A human-readable string that resolves to a UUID. Distinct from symlink — a symlink is a tree-placement item (puts an item in another location in the tree); an alias is a string lookup (lets a user address an item by name). The alias string lives in item.value; lookup is case-insensitive. Aliases exist at three scopes: personal (visible only in the personal workspace), org (shared across the org workspace), and type (defined at the type level, usually formula-generated for all items of that type). When 2+ aliases match a lookup string the adapter surfaces the full list and the user chooses. Provisional aliases (provisional: true) are computed locally at creation time and confirmed as globally unique only at merge (PR pre-flight checks for alias conflicts). Lives under the aliased item (targetId = parentId) — this is its canonical home in the tree. The adapter maintains a derived aliases lookup table for O(1) resolution; that table is never the source of truth, it is rebuilt from items.
_notes.grid: A two-dimensional sparse container — the grid is to tables what tree is to hierarchies. Only occupied cells exist as items; empty cells have no storage cost. A 1000×1000 grid with 50 values is 51 items total (one grid, fifty cells). Columns can be named (with optional type hints for Studio rendering and input form generation) or auto-indexed Excel-style (A, B, C…) — controlled by whether gridPayload.columns is an array or null. Any Kanecta item type can live in a cell: simple scalars go in item.value on the cell; rich content (images, files, formulas, functions, queries, nested trees, pipelines) goes as child items under the cell item — exactly the same mechanism as tree nodes. This makes grid a universal 2D container, not just a data table. The grid component renders each cell by first checking for child items, then falling back to item.value. Merged cells are supported via colSpan/rowSpan on cell items.
_notes.cell: A positioned slot in a grid. parentId is always the grid item UUID. row (1-based integer) and column (column key string for named grids; letter-index 'A', 'B', 'C'… for indexed grids) give the position. Simple scalar content lives in item.value. Rich content is placed as child items under the cell — the same mechanism as tree nodes; a cell can hold images, files, formulas, functions, queries, or any other Kanecta item type as children. colSpan and rowSpan enable merged cells. Because cell is a first-class item it participates in the full Kanecta item model: it can be related to other items, subscribed to, carry item_history, and be targeted by grants. This means individual cells in a shared grid can be access-controlled and change-tracked independently.
_notes.action: A named, reusable pipeline invocation — the imperative counterpart to pipeline triggers. A trigger fires automatically in response to an event (item-created, item-updated, schedule); an action is invoked explicitly by a user or agent on demand. An action item defines what to run (pipelineId), which item types it applies to (targetTypes), and what default params to pass. Studio renders applicable actions as buttons or menu items when viewing an item of a matching type. Invoking an action creates a pipeline-run with params.targetItemId injected automatically (the UUID of the item the action was invoked on), plus any caller-supplied params merged over the action's defaults. item.value is the action's display label (e.g. 'Summarise this item', 'Generate subtasks', 'Check for outdated references'). Null targetTypes means the action is a workspace-level action available everywhere, not tied to a specific item type.
_notes.query: A saved, named query expression — the Kanecta equivalent of a named query in Microsoft Access. Executes against the item graph and returns a result set displayed as a grid in Studio. Can be a simple single-type filter (all tasks assigned to alice), a parameterised expression reusable across contexts (tasks assigned to {{params.assignee}}), or a complex join across types that produces a derived result set. language determines the query dialect: 'kanecta' for the native filter/search expression (same DSL used in runIf, forEach.filter, and loopBack.if), 'sql' for direct SQL against the adapter index (full join, aggregate, and window-function power), 'graph' for Cypher queries against the AGE graph projection. When the result maps to a single known item type, returnType carries that type's UUID and Studio renders the grid with type-specific columns. When the result is a derived join across types, returnType is null and Studio infers the schema from the result columns. Executed via kanecta_run_query MCP tool or by the Studio grid renderer when a query item is viewed. params make queries reusable — each parameter is substituted into {{params.name}} placeholders in expression at execution time, and Studio generates an input form from the params definition.
_notes.subscription: A live notification channel registered against a UUID. Someone or something wants to be notified when a specific item changes. Distinct from reference — a subscription is about change notification, not external dependency or deletion protection. Lives under the watched item in the "subscriptions" aspect. payload is subscriptionPayload.
_notes.item_history: An event in an item's lifecycle. The complete sequence of item_history events for a given targetId is sufficient to replay the item's full history and reconstruct any past state — fully event-source capable. Three event types: created (first event, delta is empty, snapshot is initial state), updated (delta carries field-level from/to diffs, snapshot is state after), deleted (snapshot is final state before deletion). sequence is a monotonically increasing integer per targetId. Writing an item_history item does not itself trigger a further history event (recursion guard). parentId is the synthetic item_history type item UUID; payload.targetId identifies the item being recorded.
_notes.activity: A discrete workspace event — not a snapshot of item state, but a meaningful occurrence at the application level (item.viewed, search.performed, sync.completed, workspace.opened). Distinct from item_history: history tracks what changed, activity tracks what happened. eventType is a free-form dot-separated string. targetId is the related item or null for workspace-level events. Append-only — never updated or deleted. Not replicated to remotes by default. parentId is the synthetic activity type item UUID.
_notes.agent: A reusable executor definition for pipeline phases. Carries the runtime (claude-api, claude-code, python, or open string), model identifier, base system prompt, tool access list, and runtime-specific config. Agent items are reused across pipelines — separating identity and capability (the agent) from task-specific instructions (the pipeline phase). item.value is the agent's display name.
_notes.pipeline: An ordered sequence of agent-executed phases defining a multi-step AI workflow. A platform type — built in because Kanecta executes pipelines and the adapter, Studio, and subscription machinery all need to understand pipeline and pipeline-run items to do their jobs. The pipeline item is the definition; each invocation creates a pipeline-run child item. item.value is the pipeline's display name.
_notes.pipeline-run: The execution record of a single pipeline invocation. Created at run time and updated in place as phases execute. parentId is always the pipeline item UUID — runs accumulate as children of their pipeline in the tree. pipelineSnapshot holds the pipeline definition as it was at trigger time, ensuring in-flight runs are unaffected by later edits. parentRunId links child runs created by sub-pipeline phases back to their parent run. Subscriptions on a pipeline-run item deliver live phase progress to the Studio without polling. Tracks per-phase status, itemsProduced UUIDs, gate results, sub-pipeline child run UUIDs, and heartbeat timestamps. Append-only within a phase (itemsProduced grows); overall status field transitions through pending → running → waiting → complete/failed/cancelled.
_notes.eval: An automated test definition for a pipeline. Declares input params and a set of per-phase assertions evaluated against itemsProduced after execution. Assertion types: count (item count comparison), type (all/any items must be a declared type), payload (expression against item.payload), meta (expression against item.meta), llm-judge (agent evaluates output quality and returns PASS/FAIL with reasoning), no-regression (compare against stored golden items). parentId is the pipeline item UUID — evals live as children of their pipeline alongside runs.
_notes.eval-run: The execution record of a single eval invocation. Created when the eval runs and updated in place as assertions evaluate. parentId is the eval item UUID. Holds a reference to the pipeline-run item created for this execution and per-assertion results including verdict and reasoning for llm-judge assertions. Status: running → passed | failed | error.
_notes.reference: A declared dependency on a UUID from outside the item graph — physical (engraved, printed, QR code), external system (another database holds this UUID as a foreign key), function argument, function output, webhook, or reactive subscription. Unlike relationship, a reference asserts that the target UUID has reach beyond Kanecta itself. blockDeletion:true prevents deletion of the target until the reference is explicitly removed. Lives as a standalone item (like relationship) — targetId in payload, not constrained by parentId. The item_references derived index tracks structural references automatically; reference items cover declared/manual cases.
_notes.document: A named projection of a subtree into document form. Points at a target item via payload.targetId and declares how the tree under that item should be interpreted for export or display. Multiple document items can project the same subtree differently. roleMap.byDepth assigns a document role (heading, body, caption, etc.) to items at each tree depth; roleMap.byType overrides per item type. expandState controls how deep the tree is rendered and which subtrees are collapsed. isOrgDefault marks the canonical org-level view; fork-on-modify creates a personal copy (meta.visibility: 'private') with baseDocumentId tracking the source. parentId is the synthetic document type item UUID (universal rule). The exportMarkdown(adapter, documentId) function in kanecta-lib renders the projection as a Markdown string.
